title: Small for Dates - Intrauterine Growth Restriction
chief_complaint: 30-year-old G1P0 at 32 weeks with fundal height measuring 28cm (lagging 4 weeks). Ultrasound shows EFW <10th percentile with elevated umbilical artery Doppler PI.

hidden_data.us_growth: EFW 1,450g (<5th percentile), HC/AC ratio elevated (asymmetric IUGR)
hidden_data.doppler: Umbilical artery PI >95th percentile, MCA normal, ductus venosus normal

key_learning_points: Umbilical artery Doppler: absent/reversed end-diastolic flow = severe
Serial monitoring: NST + AFI + Dopplers q1-2 weeks
Delivery: 37 weeks for stage I, 34 weeks for stage II, 32 weeks for stage III

patient_responses.movement: Baby moving, but less than before

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! IUGR from placental insufficiency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is IUGR. The EFW <10th + abnormal Dopplers confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: IUGR: Betamethasone, serial monitoring (NST + AFI + Dopplers q1-2 weeks). Deliver at 37 weeks if stage I. If absent/reversed EDF, admit and deliver by 34 weeks. Continuous fetal monitoring in labor.
